columns.name: signal
columns.description: inline quality reading taken during the run

columns.name: outcome
columns.description: final quality score (higher = better; below 15 = rejected)

stakes.narrative: A process line must set one intensity per campaign and price its reject exposure; its historical records were taken in batches over an era in which BOTH the meter's calibration and the line's operating intensity drifted -- the line suspects its records overstate how much intensity buys, and it is paying to find out what a deliberately set intensity really does to the reject rate.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: a unit whose quality lands below the reject line of 15 is scrapped at the line's cost

shift.description: hall/period baseline (a property of when the run happens; targetable in experiments, not set per-unit)

control_surface.experiment_note: experimental readings are taken by the SAME instrument in fresh runs: they arrive in batches too (batch_id column), each batch with its own meter state
control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> table over [driver, signal, outcome] (no batch_id: batches are a property of the measurement runs, not of the process). Model the PROCESS, not the meter: observed readings include per-batch meter offsets and instrument noise; your model should reproduce true quality.
